Section.Title: Description
Section.Paragraph: The SetInitialData method supplies initial data to use when allocating the buffer. Note that the supplied memory must remain valid and accessible until the AllocateMemory method is called.

Note.Paragraph: Initial data is consumed when memory is allocated. The source memory must remain valid and unmodified until allocation occurs.

Section.Title: Usage
Section.Code: SuccessToken SetInitialData(const void* sourceBuffer, size_t sourceBufferSizeInBytes);

ArgumentList.ArgumentListEntry: The source data to use when the resource is allocated.
The size, in bytes, of the source data.

SubSection.Title: Return value
SubSection.ReturnValue: True if the operation succeeded, false otherwise. An assertion will be raised in debug builds if the result is not tested.
